Scenario.overview: Energy Industry Simple Operation Sharpshooter targets the Energy industry using expert-level skills to gain initial access, execute malicious code, and disrupt operations.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Target Artifact (Application), OS Process, Command, Network Traffic
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Code Injection, Process Injection, Command Execution (Mechanism), Network Traffic Content (Mechanism)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2019-7286 (Suggested): Apple iOS, macOS, watchOS, and tvOS contain a memory corruption vulnerability that could allow for privilege escalation.
stage.description: The attacker sends targeted emails to energy company employees with credentials, exploiting a memory corruption vulnerability in Apple devices to gain initial access.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2018-0167 (Suggested): There is a buffer overflow vulnerability in the Link Layer Discovery Protocol (LLDP) subsystem of Cisco IOS Software, Cisco IOS XE Software, and Cisco IOS XR Software which could allow an unauthenticated, adjacent attacker to cause a denial of service (DoS) condition or execute arbitrary code.
stage.description: The attacker embeds malicious code in the compromised energy company website, allowing for drive-by downloads and initial access.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2012-0391 (Suggested): The ExceptionDelegator component in Apache Struts 2 before 2.2.3.1 contains an improper input validation vulnerability that allows for remote code execution.
stage.description: The attacker exploits vulnerabilities in SCADA systems to inject malicious code and execute it remotely.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2010-0840 (Suggested): Unspecified vulnerability in the Java Runtime Environment (JRE) in Java SE component allows remote attackers to affect confidentiality, integrity, and availability via Unknown vectors.
stage.description: The attacker injects malware into energy management system processes to maintain persistence and escalate privileges.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-24: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: The attacker uses automated tools to exfiltrate sensitive data related to energy production and distribution.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker disrupts energy grid operations by disabling network traffic, causing a denial-of-service condition.

Scenario.summary: Operation Sharpshooter is a sophisticated attack campaign targeting the Energy industry, utilizing expert-level skills to gain initial access, execute malicious code, and disrupt operations.
